topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box sat on the welcome mat like a physical manifestation of my mounting frustration. I stared at the address label, the handwriting suspiciously neat, and then at the faded name of the previous occupant. Another unsolicited delivery. I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that seemed to vibrate the dusty air of the hallway. I picked up the package, the tape feeling cheap and flimsy under my fingers. "Seriously?" I muttered to the empty apartment, my jaw tightening. I took it to the mailroom, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead seeming to mock my patience. I couldn't believe I had to deal with this nonsense.

[story 2]

My shoulders slumped as I surveyed the item. It was a heavy, awkwardly shaped box, clearly addressed to someone who had long since vacated the premises. I ran a hand through my hair, feeling a familiar tightness behind my eyes. Why did everything have to be so inconvenient? I pictured the previous tenant, probably blissfully unaware of the mess they’d left behind, now apparently trying to send me junk mail. I set the box down with a deliberate, sharp thud. "This is ridiculous," I whispered, the sound brittle in the quiet apartment. I circled the box twice, a slow, agitated pacing motion, before finally deciding to summon the energy to deal with the inevitable return trip.

[story 3]

I opened the door, the sound of the delivery person’s muffled voice echoing faintly in the hallway. There it was: a package clearly meant for the former resident. I felt a distinct prickling sensation across my skin, a low-grade hum of annoyance building in my chest. I didn't bother looking closely at the contents; I just wanted it gone. I took the package with two fingers, gripping it firmly as if it were contaminated. "Just... leave it," I managed, my voice clipped and dangerously level. I carried it straight to the curb, not bothering to look back at the building, needing distance from the whole irritating situation.
